findings.description: PROP-006 written: CLAUDE.md structured refactor. Splits 441-line CLAUDE.md into ~165-line lean core + 5 reference files (VERSION-ARCHIVE.md, OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md, SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT.md, DATA-SCHEMAS.md, BUILD-AND-CHANGE.md). Per-agent reading lists ensure each agent only loads what it needs. Also fixes analyst open-issues.json ownership violation by routing issue creation through monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/ staging directory.
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md: 441 lines, ~10,800 tokens. Read by 8 agents every run. Section-by-section analysis: Version History (27L), Key Scientific Arguments (31L), wins.json Schema (30L), How to Make Changes (30L), File Map (57L), Session Setup (18L), Operator Runbook (19L), Curmudgeon Lifecycle (10L), External Problem Reporting (9L), Analyst Modes (9L), Parked Content (4L) = 244 lines needed by ≤2 agents each. Analyst writes to open-issues.json in analyst-mode0-onboarding.md:72 and analyst-normal-analysis.md:96 (git-owned, decider-only).

suggested_fix.rationale: Full proposal with lean CLAUDE.md text, all 5 reference files, analyst ownership fix, and new append-only directory classification. Ready for human review.

findings.description: Stale schedule descriptions in decider.md lines 13-14: 'Analyst (every 30min)' should be 'every 2h', 'Curmudgeon (every 10min)' should be 'every 4h'. Cosmetic — the decider doesn't use these for routing — but creates confusion if any agent reads them.
findings.evidence: decider.md:13 'every 30min', decider.md:14 'every 10min'. Actual schedules: analyst 0 */2, curmudgeon 0 */4.

suggested_fix.find:
- **Analyst** (every 30min): Deep scientific analysis, new WIN entries, expansions
- **Curmudgeon** (every 10min): Adversarial self-review
suggested_fix.replace:
- **Analyst** (every 2h): Deep scientific analysis, new WIN entries, expansions
- **Curmudgeon** (every 4h): Adversarial self-review
suggested_fix.rationale: Cosmetic fix to match actual cron schedules. Deferred to Mode 4 self-fix.
